weather_report:
Subiaco settles into a damp, reflective night. Light rain has been padding the streets and is likely to linger through late evening into the small hours, the sky a soft ash-gray curtain without a real break. Sunshine was scarce today and will be rarer still now; the UV index has been modest under the cloud and drops to nearly nothing at this hour. The air feels rinsed but not clear, with low clouds muting the horizon and turning the town’s lights into small halos on wet pavement.

It’s a night for a light waterproof layer, an umbrella you don’t mind carrying, and shoes that won’t mind a puddle. The weather is gentle rather than harsh, more sigh than storm, but it will sneak into your cuffs if you let it. If you’re out, a slow walk between awnings, a late cinema showing, or a quiet café with steamed windows suits the mood; if you’re in, it’s a good time to unspool tomorrow’s plans while the rain keeps time against the glass.
